---
title: "bpca Overview"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{bpca Overview}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

## Introduction

`bpca` provides tools for 2D and 3D biplot analysis based on principal
component analysis (PCA), including diagnostics for representation quality.

## Basic Workflow

```{r}
library(bpca)

bp <- bpca(gabriel1971)
bp
```

## Summaries

```{r}
summary(bp)
```

## Quality Diagnostics

```{r}
bp_q <- bpca(gabriel1971, var.rb = TRUE)
q <- qbpca(gabriel1971, bp_q)
head(q)
```

## 3D Reduction

```{r}
bp3 <- bpca(gabriel1971, d = 1:3)
summary(bp3)
```

## Plotting

The package supports multiple plot types for 2D and 3D objects.
Interactive plotting with `rgl` can be used in regular sessions:

```{r, eval = FALSE}
plot(bp)
plot(bp3)
plot(bp3, rgl.use = TRUE)
```
